title: Total RNA extraction (TRIzol)
description: Single-step TRIzol total RNA extraction with chloroform phase separation, per the Thermo Fisher TRIzol Reagent user guide.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Isolate total RNA from cells or tissue in one tube using TRIzol (phenol/guanidine) lysis, chloroform phase separation, and isopropanol precipitation. Work RNase-free throughout.

## Materials

| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| TRIzol Reagent | 1 mL per 50-100 mg tissue, or per 1x10^5 to 1x10^7 cells |
| Chloroform | 0.2 mL per 1 mL TRIzol |
| Isopropanol | 0.5 mL per 1 mL TRIzol |
| 75% ethanol (in RNase-free water) | 1 mL per 1 mL TRIzol |
| RNase-free water | 20-50 uL |
| RNase-free glycogen carrier (optional, small samples) | 5-10 ug |

## Steps

1. Lyse the sample in TRIzol (1 mL per 50-100 mg tissue, or 1 mL per 1x10^5 to 1x10^7 cells). Homogenize or pipette to fully lyse.
2. Incubate 5 min to let nucleoprotein complexes dissociate.
3. Add 0.2 mL chloroform per 1 mL TRIzol. Cap and shake.
4. Incubate 2-3 min.
5. Centrifuge 15 min at 12,000 x g, 4 C. Transfer the upper colorless aqueous phase (RNA) to a fresh tube, angling the tube at 45 degrees. Do not disturb the interphase.
6. Optional (small samples): add 5-10 ug RNase-free glycogen as a carrier.
7. Add 0.5 mL isopropanol per 1 mL TRIzol used. Incubate 10 min at room temperature (15-30 C).
8. Centrifuge 10 min at 12,000 x g, 4 C. RNA forms a white gel-like pellet.
9. Discard the supernatant. Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL 75% ethanol per 1 mL TRIzol. Vortex briefly.
10. Centrifuge 5 min at 7,500 x g, 4 C. Discard the wash.
11. Air-dry the pellet 5-10 min. Do not let it dry completely.
12. Resuspend in 20-50 uL RNase-free water. Incubate at 55-60 C for 10-15 min.

## Expected result

Total RNA with A260/A280 about 2.0. Check 28S and 18S rRNA integrity on a denaturing or quick agarose gel.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- Use RNase-free tubes and tips and decontaminate surfaces (e.g. RNaseZap).
- The user guide specifies a single 75% ethanol wash. Do not over-dry the pellet or it will not redissolve.
- Low A260/A230 (phenol/salt carryover): repeat the 75% ethanol wash.

## Safety

TRIzol contains phenol and guanidine and is corrosive and toxic. Chloroform is toxic and volatile. Work in a fume hood with nitrile gloves. Dispose of as phenolic/halogenated waste. Do not combine guanidine waste with bleach.

## Source

Adapted from the Thermo Fisher / Invitrogen TRIzol Reagent User Guide (Pub. No. MAN0001271).
https://documents.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/LSG/manuals/trizol_reagent.pdf
